description: The Komala Toilers Party (Zehmetkêshan, meaning 'toilers' or 'labourers' in Kurdish) is the third major faction to emerge from the historic Komala organisation, distinct from both the [Komala Party of Iranian Kurdistan](/orgs/komala-party-of-iranian-kurdistan) led by Abdullah Mohtadi and the Communist Party of Iran-Komala faction led by Ibrahim Alizadeh. It represents a current that broke away during the post-2000 reorganisation of the Kurdish left, maintaining a Marxist orientation while charting an independent course from both of the larger Komala factions. Like the other Komala branches, it operates in exile from Iraqi Kurdistan and advocates Kurdish self-determination within a federal, democratic Iran.
editorial_notes: Third Komala faction. Distinct from Mohtadi's independent Komala and Alizadeh's CPI-Komala. Emerged from post-2000 splits.
